evals.prompt: I need to add an optional 'estimatedDuration' field (Int16) to the TaskEntity so users can estimate how long a task will take. Walk me through the migration.
evals.expected_output: Agent should assess this as a lightweight, CloudKit-compatible migration, explain the steps to create a new model version, add the optional attribute, update entity extensions, and test
expectations: The agent correctly identifies this as a lightweight migration (adding optional attribute)
The agent confirms this is CloudKit-compatible (optional attribute is additive)
The agent reads the current Core Data model to understand existing TaskEntity structure
The agent instructs the user to create a new model version in Xcode (not edit programmatically)
The agent explains what code changes are needed in entity extension files
The agent warns this is an L2 action requiring user confirmation before modifying .xcdatamodeld

evals.prompt: I want to rename the 'isCompleted' field to 'completionStatus' on TaskEntity and change it from Bool to Int16 so I can track partial completion. How do I do this safely?
evals.expected_output: Agent should flag this as NOT CloudKit-compatible, explain that renaming and type changes require a mapping model, and suggest alternative approaches like adding a new field instead
expectations: The agent identifies this as NOT a lightweight migration (rename + type change)
The agent warns this is NOT CloudKit-compatible (non-additive change)
The agent suggests an alternative approach — adding a new optional field instead of renaming
The agent explains the risks of breaking existing CloudKit sync
The agent does NOT attempt to edit .xcdatamodeld files directly
